summary:
## This month in numbers
Cambodia experienced its 24th warmest April on record in 2026, with an average temperature of 29.38°C, an anomaly of +0.7°C compared to the 1961–1990 baseline. The period of February to April 2026 also ranked as the 25th warmest on record, with an average of 28.56°C, also +0.7°C above the baseline. Globally, April 2026 was the 2nd warmest April on record for land temperatures, with an anomaly of +1.1°C, while the February–April 2026 period was also the 2nd warmest on record globally, with an anomaly of +1.2°C.

## What changed
Cambodia's recent temperatures reflect a broader warming trend, with the latest full-year average temperature in 2025 reaching 28.31°C, making it the warmest year on record since 1941. This contributes to a long-term warming trend of +1.20°C compared to the 1961–1990 baseline. While Cambodia's April anomaly of +0.7°C places it 200th out of 234 regions globally for the latest month, the global land temperature ranking as the 2nd warmest on record highlights the widespread nature of current warming. For the three-month period, Cambodia ranked 219th out of 234 regions.

## What’s driving change?
The elevated temperatures in Cambodia are influenced by the broader global warming trend, with the country experiencing a significant long-term increase in average temperature. A drought event, which began in January 2026 and continued through May, represents 100% of the drought events logged for Cambodia over the past 12 months, indicating an unusual concentration. This drought, alongside persistent high pressure and weak air circulation, exacerbated heatwave conditions in April, with several provinces experiencing temperatures between 37°C and 41°C, exceeding average annual temperatures for the month. This aligns with the warming driver of monsoon disruption, as erratic rainfall and extended dry-season heat contribute to higher temperatures. The country is also highly vulnerable to the urban heat island effect, particularly in its urban centers, which can amplify high temperatures. More information on extreme weather events can be found at /extreme-weather.

## Looking ahead
Above-normal temperatures are expected to continue rising in Cambodia through May and June, indicating a warmer-than-usual season.
